revision.comment: Updating the Commons category from "Category:Yata Otsuka" to "Category:Yata Otsuka Kofun" to avoid a category redirect
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]
[[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kurashiki]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]]

Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

Kofun[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

Kofun[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:qq]][[Category:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
