{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]
Yata Ōtsuka Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]

These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}} [[file:箭田大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|right|thumb|300px|Grave goods from Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]
Yata Ōtsuka Kofun

It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]
Yata Ōtsuka Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]
Yata Ōtsuka Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[:en:Ibara Railway Ibara Line|Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]
Yata Ōtsuka Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including [[Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[:en:Kibinomakibi Station|Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[:en:Ibara Railway Ibara Line|Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]
Yata Ōtsuka Kofun

[[sn:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]]}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[:en:Takahashi River|Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[:en:Kibinomakibi Station|Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[:en:Ibara Railway Ibara Line|Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]
Yata Ōtsuka Kofun

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603302]] Wikidata name: Yata Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.635605555556|N|133.68132222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[:en:Takahashi River|Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[:en:Kibinomakibi Station|Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[:en:Ibara Railway Ibara Line|Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603302]] Wikidata name: Yata Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | native_name =箭田大塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yataotsuka 02.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yata Ōtsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.635605555556|N|133.68132222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|8.18|N|133|40|52.76|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Yata Ōtsuka Kofun'''|箭田大塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabi-chō]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurashiki, Okayama|Kurashiki]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929, with the area under protection expanded in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211050|title=箭田大塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yata Ōtsuka Kofun is located on the north bank of the Oda River near the confluence with the [[:en:Takahashi River|Takahashi River]]. It was [[archaeological excavation| excavated]] in 1901, during which time a large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery, as well as iron swords, horse harnesses, metal rings, and ''[[magatama]]'', were discovered. These artifacts are now in the collection of the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal passage chamber made by precisely combining huge stones. The total length of the burial chamber including the passage is 19.1 meters, with the burial chamber itself measuring 8.4 by 3 meters, with a height of 3.8 meters. Inside the burial chamber are three interlocking sarcophaguses, which are believed to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[:en:Kibinomakibi Station|Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[:en:Ibara Railway Ibara Line|Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yata Otsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5508.htm Kurashiki City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260861.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page]

It was presumed that this tumulus was a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above, or a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|Hotategata-kofun}}, but a subsequent excavation in 1983 discovered a moat and revealed the shape to be a round ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}})-style tumulus with a diameter of 54 meters and height of 7 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 23-minute walk from [[:en:Kibinomakibi Station|Kibinomakibi Station]] on the [[:en:Ibara Railway Ibara Line|Ibara Railway Ibara Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
